title: tetraclinis aphylla
intro: Tetraclinis aphylla, also known as the Arborio pine, is a coniferous tree native to the island of Cyprus and the surrounding region, belonging to the family Cupressaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties attributed to its essential oil, which contains compounds such as alpha-pinene, limonene, and beta-pinene. The health benefits of Tetraclinis aphylla include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, improve immune function, and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Tetraclinis aphylla can be found in the form of essential oil, extracts, and infusions, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
